title: picea glauca subsp. engelmannii
intro: Picea glauca subsp. engelmannii, also known as Engelmann spruce, is a coniferous tree species native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The medicinal properties of Engelmann spruce are attributed to its essential oils, which contain compounds like alpha-pinene, beta-pinene, and limonene, that have antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate stress, making it a popular ingredient in natural remedies for colds, coughs, and other respiratory conditions. Engelmann spruce can be prepared into herbal teas, essential oils, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation, soothe sore muscles, and support immune function.
